summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a solid 1981 show highlighted by stellar NFA‑Wharf Rat and tight Scarlet > Fire jams.
review: The matrix source delivers crisp, clean sound that lets the band’s 1981 energy shine – Brent’s keyboards glow, Jerry’s guitar is on point (even if his vocals slip a few times), and the rhythm section locks in.  Highlights include a soaring NFA‑Wharf Rat sequence, tight Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, and a flowing Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World, while the occasional misstep (short encore, a rough Alabama) doesn’t detract from an overall strong performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating ~4.28 with >30 reviews, most praising both sound quality and musical highlights; weighting toward show quality (70%) keeps the score in the strong‑good range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as clean, crisp, superb matrix sound; occasional vocal flubs and a brief cut on "Loser" noted, but overall balanced mix with clear audience ambience.

standout_songs: Not Fade Away > Space > Not Fade Away
Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical early‑80s build – a tight first set that ramps into an intense second set; strong song selection for 1981 with the usual mix of ballads, rockers and exploratory jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work praised as energetic and clean; vocals noted as generally good but with occasional flubs on this night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Fender Rhodes intro and B‑3 playing receive repeated praise; his backing vocals also highlighted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Steady, propulsive drumming noted throughout, especially the post‑drums space leading into NFA.
